title: Overloading operators using member functions

exercises.title: Point2D Addition
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal

Implement `operator+` as a member function of a `Point2D` class so that adding two points produces a new point whose coordinates are the sums of the operands' coordinates.

## Input

Four integers on a single line separated by spaces: `x1 y1 x2 y2` (each between -1000 and 1000).

## Output

A single line in the format `(x, y)` representing the sum of the two points, followed by a newline.

## Requirements

- Overload `operator+` as a **member function** (not a friend or free function).
- The member function should be `const`-qualified.
- Do not add any other operator overloads.

exercises.title: Fraction Multiplication
exercises.prompt_md:
## Goal

Implement `operator*` as a member function of a `Fraction` class. Multiplying two fractions should produce a new fraction in **reduced form**.

## Input

Four integers on a single line separated by spaces: `num1 den1 num2 den2` (numerators between -100 and 100, denominators between 1 and 100).

## Output

A single line in the format `num/den` representing the product in reduced form, followed by a newline. If the result is negative, the negative sign should be on the numerator only.

## Requirements

- Overload `operator*` as a **member function** (not a friend or free function).
- Write a helper to compute the greatest common divisor and use it to reduce the fraction in the constructor.
- The member function should be `const`-qualified.

test_cases.label: Result reduces to whole number fraction
